name: apierr-keygen-privkey-parse-whitespace
doc: APIERR-channel fail fixture. Two routers joined by one primary link — structurally identical to the single-primary-link SUCCESS fixture, so it passes schema AND semantic validation cleanly (the validator never inspects key MATERIAL, only the topology shape; the apierr channel below carries the sole code, verdict.validator is empty). node-alpha's wireguard_private_key carries an embedded SPACE, which is NOT valid base64.StdEncoding: Go's wgtypes.ParseKey (via render.GenerateKeys case-a ParseAndNormalize) rejects it with 'illegal base64 data' and the compile error rides through as apierr.CodeKeygenPrivkeyParse (keygen_privkey_parse_failed). A SPACE is used deliberately (NOT a newline): Go's StdEncoding decoder IGNORES embedded '\n'/'\r' and would accept those, but rejects space/tab/other non-alphabet bytes — so a space is a byte BOTH Go and the TS keygen seam reject, pinning the faithful base64 decoder. This pins the apierr failure channel through the key pre-pass (NOT the allocator), complementing the transit-pool fixture.
